title: MetaHeritage Webinar #6: Heritage Innovation Meets Investment – Connecting Culture and Capital
content:
Online, June 25, 12-1pm CET
Cultural heritage is increasingly recognized not only as a societal value but also as a driver of innovation, sustainable development, and economic opportunity. The upcoming MetaHeritage Webinar 6, titled “Heritage Innovation and Investment: Connecting Culture and Capital”, will explore exactly … Continue reading →

title: NOW LIVE on Europeana: EUreka3D-XR wrap-up post
content:
Featuring EUreka3D Initiative summary video
As EUreka3D-XR approaches its final stage, we can start to draw conclusions from a fruitful 18 months and are delighted to share the project’s work, achievements and resources: “EUreka3D-XR: exploring cultural heritage through 3D digitisation and XR experiences” is now … Continue reading →

title: EUreka3D-XR at 2and3D Photography
content:
21 May 2026, Rijksmuseum (Amsterdam)
On May 21st, on the second day of the 2and3D Photography conference held at Rijksmuseum in Amsterdam, David Iglésias Franch (CRDI/Photoconsortium) and Axelle Vanmaele (meemoo) had the opportunity to deliver a presentation in the context of EUreka3D-XR project titled Challenges … Continue reading →

title: EUreka3D-XR Final Event held successfully in Cyprus

title: Navigating AI, Authenticity and Ethical Challenges in Heritage Collections
content:
26 June 2026, in Brussels and online
Registrations are open for the EUreka3D-XR hybrid capacity building event in Brussels on 26 June 2026: Navigating AI, Authenticity and Ethical Challenges in Heritage Collections This interdisciplinary conference explores the intersection of AI, authenticity, and ethics in heritage collections. title: Metaheritage Webinar #5: Exploring EIT Culture & Creativity Support for European SMEs
content:
June 4, 10-11AM
The MetaHeritage Webinar Series continues with its fifth session, focusing on the opportunities offered by EIT Culture & Creativity for European SMEs and organisations working across cultural heritage, tourism, and the creative industries. title: Twin it! Part II: high-level political Closing Event marking a Milestone for Europe’s 3D Cultural Heritage Strategy
content:
12 May 2026, Brussels - invitation only event

title: Cross-Fertilisation process on the topic of Regenerative Tourism Concept
content:
SECreTOUR Networking activities
  Regenera4MED is a project co-funded by the Interreg Euro-MED Programme of the European Commission to help Mediterranean regions shifting from sustainable to regenerative tourism, including restoring ecosystems, culture and community well-being. The Regenera4MED project launched an interesting cross-fertilisation initiative … Continue reading →
